questions.Subject: Export The Data
questions.Description: Export settings are now complete. Pressing the 'Next >' button will begin the Database Export process.
questions.Question: Press 'Next >' to begin the data export process.

questions.Subject: Database Export
questions.Description:
The Database Export mechanism allows the export of parts and marketing data from the Right-Proposal™ parts database and/or equipment database to Excel spreadsheets or text files.
The data that is exported is formatted so that it may be imported back into RSU.  See documentation for details.  This ensures that the data exported is complete with all required fields correctly defined. 
You can export the details of your parts and equipment from your Right-Proposal™ parts database.  You can also export pricing data that show the prices of existing parts.
questions.Question: Press <Next> to advance to the next page

questions.Subject: What kind of data
questions.Description:
Database import supports import of two types of data:
1) Completed Right-Suite Excel "Parts Templates" with correctly formatted data.
2) Manufacturer or distributor Excel price lists containing updated pricing information only.
questions.Question: Please select an export option from the choices below
questions.AnswerPrompts:
EEXCEL.ET.RightSuite|
EEXCEL.ET.PriceList

questions.Subject: Choosing What To Export
questions.Description: The Excel Export mechanism supports the export of parts and equipment data. If equipment and parts data are both to be exported the equipment must be represented in the parts data using the same manufacturer code and part number or they will be ommitted from the export.
questions.Question: Please select which data you are importing from the choices below
questions.AnswerPrompts:
Include Right-Proposal™ parts data|
Include equipment data

questions.Subject: Finished
questions.Description: Congratulations, you have finished setting up your system. You may return to this wizard at any time by selecting it from the main menu.
questions.Question: Press 'Finish' to save settings and exit the wizard

questions.Subject: Choose Project
questions.Description: You can start a new project, modify an existing project, or modify the current one.
questions.Question: New, existing, or current project?

questions.Subject: Setup Complete
questions.Description: This wizard has now completed, you may review your settings at this time by use of the back button, or press the finish button to exit the wizard

questions.Subject: Import The Data
questions.Description:
Import settings are now complete. Pressing the 'Next >' button will begin the Database Import process. As data files are required you will be prompted for them. If all the data (spreadsheet, images, etc.) are in the same folder they will automatically be selected. 

Whenever a required file cannot be found a dialog will appear prompting for the missing file. Simply select the folder containing the missing file and press <OK>. Pressing <Cancel> will skip the file.
questions.Question: Press 'Next >' to begin the data import process.

questions.Subject: Database Import
questions.Description:
The Database Import mechanism allows the import of parts and marketing data from Excel spreadsheets or text files into the Right-Proposal® parts database and/or equipment database. The imported data may be merged with or replace existing data.
Data to be imported must be formatted using the correct template. See documentation for details.  This ensures that the data imported is complete with all required fields correctly defined. 
You can import detailed data that adds parts and equipment to your Right-Proposal® parts database.  You can also import pricing data only that will update the prices of existing parts.
To verify that the Excel spreadsheet for the detailed data import is correct, make sure that each table (one on each tab) can be selected using the names box in the upper left corner of the spreadsheet. Only data "selected" this way will be imported.  This is not required for importing price updates.
questions.Question: Press <Next> to advance to the next page

questions.Subject: What kind of data
questions.Description:
Database import supports import of three types of data:
1) Completed Excel "Parts Templates" with correctly formatted data.
2) Manufacturer or distributor Excel price lists containing updated pricing information only.
3) Manufacturer or distributor comma-separated price list containing updated pricing information only.
questions.Question: Please select an import option from the choices below
questions.AnswerPrompts:
IEXCEL.IT.RightSuite|
IEXCEL.IT.PriceList|
IEXCEL.IT.PriceListCSV

questions.Subject: Choosing How To Update
questions.Description: There are several choices for how to perform the update. The imported data can be merged with the existing database or used to replace the existing data. When replacing the existing data only data for the tables present in the imported spreadsheet will be replaced, other tables will remain intact.
questions.Question: Please select an import option from the choices below
questions.AnswerPrompts:
IEXCEL.DR.Merge|
IEXCEL.DR.Mfg

questions.Subject: Choosing How To Update
questions.Description: There are several choices for how to perform the update. The imported data can be merged with the existing database or used to replace the existing data. When replacing the existing data only data for the tables present in the imported spreadsheet will be replaced, other tables will remain intact.
questions.Question: Please select your import option from the choices below
questions.AnswerPrompts:
IEXCEL.DR.Merge|
IEXCEL.DR.Cat|
IEXCEL.DR.Mfg

questions.Subject: Choosing What To Update
questions.Description: The Excel Import mechanism supports the import of parts and equipment data. If equipment and parts data are both to be imported the equipment must be represented in the parts data using the same manufacturer code and part number or they will be ommitted from the import.
questions.Question: Please select which data you are importing from the choices below
questions.AnswerPrompts:
Include Right-Proposal® parts data|
Include equipment data

questions.Subject: Fuel Costs
questions.Description: Right-$™ uses fuel cost information to estimate annual heating and cooling costs, and is used to determine cost savings over time when newer, more efficient equipment is used to replace older worn units. You may enter the cost basis for fuels used in these calculations on this page.

questions.Subject: Energy Recovery Ventilator
questions.Description: Use energy recovery ventilation (ERV) for efficient air to air exchange. This helps reduce build up of humidity in enclosed systems without significant energy loss.
questions.Question: Please select from the following options:
questions.AnswerPrompts: GA.None|ERV.1200|ERV.1500|ERV.2100|ERV.2000Plus

questions.Subject: Air Quality - Filtration
questions.Description: Use HEPA room air purifiers to reduce allergens in the air. HEPA stands for 'High Efficiency Particulate Air', which captures up to 99.97% os particles in the air, up to 0.3 microns in size. A good air purifier uses HEPA filtration.
questions.Question: Which of these best describes your air filtration needs:
questions.AnswerPrompts: GA.None|AQ.Elec|AQ.Media|AQ.Pleated

questions.Subject: Garage heating
questions.Description: Keep your garage or workshop comfortable, regardless of outside weather conditions.  Protect your vehicles from engine wear; improve vehicle life, fuel economy and reliability.
questions.Question: Please select from the following options:
questions.AnswerPrompts: GA.None|GARHTG.30|GARHTG.45|GARHTG.60|GARHTG.75

questions.Subject: Dehumidification
questions.Description: Controlling the amount of moisture in the air is necessary for your family's health. Too much moisture in the air can lead to mold, dampness and property damage. A Humidistat can be used to help control summer humidity.
questions.Question: Please select a humidity control option:
questions.AnswerPrompts: GA.None|DHUM.ThermStat|DHUM.Other

HUM.SmallFan|HUM.LargeFan|

questions.Subject: Service Plan
questions.Description: Service plans are available to meet every budget. Control your costs by selecting a suitable service plan to keep your system in efficient running condition.
questions.Question: Please select from the following service plans:
questions.AnswerPrompts: GA.None|SP.Annual|SP.Callout

questions.Subject: Air Quality - UV Lights
questions.Description: Dust, dirt, mold and other substances can accumulate on any heating and cooling component, reducing airflow by as much as 9%*. UV Lights help minimize accumulation, letting your system operate at its peak efficiency levels. Mold is one of the more common allergens. A 1999 Mayo Clinic study found that mold was the culprit in nearly all chronic sinus infections.
questions.Question: Which of these best describes your needs:
questions.AnswerPrompts: GA.None|AQUV.Single|AQUV.Dual|AQUV.Coil

questions.Subject: Warranty
questions.Description: Once the factory warranty expires, you have nothing to protect you from unexpected, costly repairs. With an extended  warranty, you can protect yourself from these costs for years after the factory warranty expires.
questions.Question: Please select from the following warranty programs:
questions.AnswerPrompts: WO.Manf|WO.Extended5|WO.Extended10

questions.Subject: Zone Controls
questions.Description: Electronic zone controls allow you to control the heating and cooling in different areas of your home independantly of one another.
questions.Question: Which of these best describes your zoning needs:
questions.AnswerPrompts: GA.None|ZC.Economy|ZC.Performance|ZC.Ultimate

questions.Subject: Available Catalogs
questions.Description: Right-Catalog® Internet Update supports the download of manufacturer and distributor catalogs.
questions.Question: Please select which catalogs to download and press next.

questions.Subject: Begin the update
questions.Description:
Update settings are now complete. Pressing the <Update Now> button will connect to the Right-Catalog® server and begin the update process. This process may take some time so please be prepared to be patient and wait for the process to complete.

If data transfer problems occur with the update it may be repeated without any problems. Normally it will pick up from where it left off. If errors occur repeatedly please check your internet connection and only contact Wrightsoft technical support after verifying that the problem is only with the Right-Catalog® server connection, and not your internet connection.
questions.Question: Press <Update Now> to connect to the Right-Catalog® server.

questions.Subject: Right-Catalog® Internet Update
questions.Description:
The Right-Catalog® Internet Update mechanism allows the import of parts and marketing data from the Right-Catalog® central server into the Right-Proposal® parts database and/or equipment database. The imported data is merged with or replaces existing data used by the program as necessary.

Right-Catalog® data is verified at the server and should contain correct data. If after verification you find errors in this data please report these immediately to Wrightsoft technical support.

Only catalogs that you have permission to obtain will be available to you in this process. This is based upon your Right-Suite® license and customer number. If you believe you are not getting the appropriate data please contact Wrightsoft technical suppoort.
questions.Question: Press <Next> to advance to the next page

questions.Subject: Choosing What To Update
questions.Description: Right-Catalog® Internet Update supports the import of parts and equipment data. For correct operation of Right-Proposal® it is recommended that both parts and equipment are updated at the same time.
questions.Question: Please select which data you are importing from the choices below
questions.AnswerPrompts:
Delete all records before download of catalog|
Include Right-Proposal® parts data|
Overwite local list prices with catalog data|
Include custom wizards and questionnaires|
Include equipment data

questions.Subject: Right-Catalog®
questions.Description:
The next step is to download the latest pricing information from the Right-Catalog® server. This will fetch all relevant pricing information from your distributor(s) and manufacturer(s) as appropriate.

The Right-Catalog® Internet Update mechanism allows the import of parts and marketing data from the Right-Catalog® central server into the Right-Proposal® parts database and/or equipment database. The imported data is merged with or replaces existing data used by the program as necessary.
questions.Question: Press next to go to the Right-Catalog® wizard

questions.Subject: Select Report Package
questions.Description: You can select the default report package that will be available for this project.
questions.Question: Please select the default report set that you want to for this project:
questions.AnswerPrompts: Report package

questions.Subject: STOP!
questions.Description: This wizard can progress no further without purchase of Right-Sales®. Please contact your Wrightsoft software vendor for more information about this exciting new feature.
questions.Question: Press cancel to exit this wizard

questions.Subject: Custom Settings
questions.Description: The first step is to customize the look and feel of your system. This includes selecting your company logo, background page, videos etc. All these help provide a custom look and feel to your right sales software.
questions.Question: Press next to begin customization

questions.Subject: Library Setup
questions.Description: You can define combinations of selections and save them in a library.  You can then select the entire combination at once.

Snowmelt preferences

questions.Subject: Right-Proposal®
questions.Description:
After setup is complete the Right-Proposal® proposal library setup program will run.

Complete instructions for entering your parts may be found in the User's Manual.

A quick summary may also be found in the Quick-Start Manual in the  "Parts Library" section.

For your convenience please encourage your distributor to participate in the Right-Catalog® program.
questions.Question: Press next to continue

questions.Subject: Pricing
questions.Description: To obtain accurate pricing for your proposals it is neccesary to have at least one distributor catalog with correct pricing information. If your distributor is a Right-Catalog® participant you may use the Internet update option to easily download up-to-date pricing information. Otherwise it must be entered manually using the Right-Proposal™ library.
questions.Question: Please choose pricing information preference
questions.AnswerPrompts:
I would like to use Right-Catalog® to get pricing information over the Internet|
I will enter pricing information manually using Right-Proposal®

questions.Subject: Setup Right-Sales®
questions.Description: The next step is to setup the Right-Sales® database.
questions.Question: Press next to begin setup  of Right-Sales®

questions.Subject: Create a default template
questions.Description:
The next step allows you to set up a default template.

When you create a default project template, the data that you enter will be entered automatically whenever you start Right-Suite® Universal.  This can save a lot of time and effort when you are creating a new project.
questions.Question: Press next to begin setup of the default template

questions.Subject: Setup Type
questions.Description: Select the type of setup you would like to perform and click the 'Begin' button to proceed.
questions.AnswerPrompts: SUWIZ.Express|SUWIZ.Template|SUWIZ.Complete

questions.Subject: System Type
questions.Description: Selecting the system type will help the program more effectively determine what choices are appropriate for this job. Choose the system type that most closely describes the system to be installed or sold.
questions.Question: Please select a system type:
questions.AnswerPrompts:
Base system|
Investment1|
Investment2|
Investment3

questions.Subject: System Type
questions.Description: Selecting the system type will help the program more effectively determine what choices are appropriate for this job. Choose the system type that most closely describes the system to be installed or sold.
questions.Question: Please select a system type:
questions.AnswerPrompts: System type

questions.Subject: Create Template
questions.Description: You can create a new template, modify an existing template, or modify the current one.
questions.Question: New, existing, or current template?

questions.Subject: Finished Template Setup
questions.Description: Congratulations, you have finished setting up your template. To save as the default template press the "Finish" button now, to choose a custom template to save to select the appropriate option below and press "Finish".
questions.Question: Select template save option and press 'Finish' to save your settings
questions.AnswerPrompts:
SAVEAS.DEFAULTTEMPLATE|
SAVEAS.TEMPLATE

questions.Subject: Library Details
questions.Description: This wizard page displays details of your library item allowing you to edit the item.

questions.Subject: Building Materials Library
questions.Description: Select the appropriate values to represent this set of building materials. Click on each building material description to change that material.  Selecting inappropriate settings, or contraditory locations may result in unpredictable HVAC calculations.

questions.Subject: Building Types Library
questions.Description: Select the appropriate values to represent this building type. You may change the number of levels in the building and name them appropriately. Floor plan and picture selection is not yet available.

questions.Subject: Hydronics Library
questions.Description: Select the appropriate baseboard data to represent this hydronic preference. Selecting inappropriate settings, or contraditory locations may result in unpredictable HVAC calculations.

questions.Subject: Indoor Conditions Library
questions.Description: Select the appropriate values to represent this set of indoor conditions. Selecting inappropriate settings, or contraditory locations may result in unpredictable HVAC calculations.

questions.Subject: Report Package Library
questions.Description: Select the appropriate reports to represent this report package. Select the reports that you want to be printed.

questions.Subject: Custom Construction Details
questions.Description: Select the appropriate settings to define this custom construction. Selecting inappropriate settings may result in inaccurate HVAC calculations.

questions.Subject: Duct Sizes Library - Rectangular Duct Sizes
questions.Description: Select the appropriate values to represent this library entry. Only the rectangular duct sizes that you select here will be used during the duct size calculations that this library entry is used for. These entries are selected into duct preferences for use in your project.

questions.Subject: Duct Preferences Library
questions.Description: Select the appropriate values to represent this set of duct preferences. Selecting inappropriate settings may result in inaccurate duct calculations.

questions.Subject: Load Preferences Library
questions.Description: Select the appropriate values to represent this set of load preferences. Selecting inappropriate settings may result in inaccurate load calculations.

questions.Subject: Radiant Panel Preferences Library
questions.Description: Select the appropriate values to represent this set of radiant panel preferences. Selecting inappropriate settings may result in inaccurate radiant panel calculations.

questions.Subject: Snow Melting Preferences Library
questions.Description: Select the appropriate values to represent this set of snowmelting preferences. Selecting inappropriate settings may result in inaccurate snow melt calculations.

questions.Subject: Weather Locations Library
questions.Description: Select the appropriate weather cities to represent this weather location. Selecting inappropriate settings, or contraditory locations may result in unpredictable HVAC calculations.

questions.Subject: Custom Glazing Details
questions.Description: Select the appropriate settings to define this custom glazing type. Selecting inappropriate settings may result in inaccurate HVAC calculations.

questions.Subject: Duct Sizes Library - Round Duct Sizes
questions.Description: Select the appropriate values to represent this library entry. Only the round duct sizes that you select here will be used during the duct size calculations that this library entry is used for. These entries are selected into duct preferences for use in your project.

questions.Subject: Duct Preferences Library - High Velocity Fittings
questions.Description: Select the appropriate fittings to represent this set of duct preferences. Selecting inappropriate settings may result in inaccurate duct calculations.

questions.Subject: Weather Locations Library
questions.Description: Listed below are the outdoor design conditions for this weather location. The default values presented are typical for the location selected.

questions.Subject: Duct Preferences Library - Fittings
questions.Description: Select the appropriate fittings to represent this set of duct preferences. Selecting inappropriate settings may result in inaccurate duct calculations.

questions.Subject: Custom Constructions Library
questions.Description: Click Finish below to exit from the custom constructions library wizard.

questions.Subject: Duct Sizes Library
questions.Description: Click Finish below to exit from the duct sizes library wizard.

questions.Subject: Library Setup
questions.Description:
This wizard is used to create and manage the user library. You may use this wizard to manage the library, including addition of new library entries, deleting unused entries, or editing existing entries. The wizard may also be used to select the current item to be used in your project.

User libraries are a convenient way to save and organise groups of common settings. These can be recalled simply by selecting the appropriate library item using this wizard. In most cases these settings can be overidden on a project by project basis.

The user library concept allows new projects to be created quickly and easily after an initial few moments of setup time.

The user libraries can be used in either wizard mode, or classic mode. In either case the items to be set up are the same and there is no loss of functionality in either case.
questions.Question: Press the "Next" button to advance to the next page of the wizard

answers.Benefits: Air treatment for allergies, ashma or upper respiratory difficulties|Kills 87% of airborn bacteria.

answers.Description: Single Lamp Ultraviolet Air Treatment system
answers.Benefits: Air treatment for allergies, ashma or upper respiratory difficulties|Kills most airborn bacteria

Fairly important - balance initial cost with running costs
